line_preview: #Use the dropdown menu at the bottom of this script to navigate between tests.
#All sample sizes are the TOTAL required sample size
# At times you may only have a p-value or sample size, and you need either the test statistic or say a SE for power calculations.
# These are general functions that can be used anywhere below to back calculate a t-value or z-value from a p-value.

z <- qnorm(1 - p_value/2) #assumes a two-tailed p-value (to use one-tailed p_value remove the /2 part). Have set up to always return a positive z-vaule, change that if appropriate by taking out the '1-'

t <- qt(p = p_value/2, df = df, lower.tail = F) # assumes two-tailed p-value (to use one-tailed p_value remove the /2 part). Have set up to always return positive t-value (remove lower.tail = F to change that)
SSRP_75 <- ceiling(pwr.r.test(n = NULL, r = r * .75, sig.level = .05, power = .9, alternative = 'two.sided')$n)
SSRP_50 <- ceiling(pwr.r.test(n = NULL, r = r * .5, sig.level = .05, power = .9, alternative = 'two.sided')$n)
dar_threshold <- ceiling(pwr.r.test(n = NULL, r = r, sig.level = .05, power = .5, alternative = 'two.sided')$n)
#### between subjects t-test, homogenous variance, replication will use equal Ns; ####
### Do not use for t-tests from regressions or follow-up contrasts from ANOVAs ###
# if you have the t-value (which is the ncp input for the ci.smd function below) - if don't have n per group, just the total N, then assume equal ns
d_sample <- ci.smd(ncp = t, n.1 = , n.2 = , conf.level = .95)$smd
# if you only have means and sds
SSRP_75 <- ceiling(pwr.t.test(d = d_sample * .75, n = NULL, sig.level = .05, power = .9, type = 'two.sample', alternative = 'two.sided')$n) * 2 #total required sample size
SSRP_50 <- ceiling(pwr.t.test(d = d_sample * .50, n = NULL, sig.level = .05, power = .9, type = 'two.sample', alternative = 'two.sided')$n) * 2 #total required sample size
dar_threshold <- ceiling(pwr.t.test(d = d_sample, n = NULL, sig.level = .05, power = .5, type = 'two.sample', alternative = 'two.sided')$n) * 2 #total required sample size
#### tukey's follow-up contrast, between subjects, equal ns in all groups ####
# if you have the p-value from the tukey's comparison
total_groups <-     #total between subjects group in the ANOVA that the follow-up tests are being applied to
dfs <-        #this is the dfw or dfe from the between subjects ANOVA
SSRP_75 <- ceiling(pwr.f2.test(u = 1, v = NULL, f2 = f2_contrast * .75^2, sig.level = .05, power = .9)$v) + 1 + 1 # total required sample size
SSRP_50 <- ceiling(pwr.f2.test(u = 1, v = NULL, f2 = f2_contrast * .5^2, sig.level = .05, power = .9)$v) + 1 + 1  #total required sample size
dar_threshold <- ceiling(pwr.f2.test(u = 1, v = NULL, f2 = f2_contrast, sig.level = .05, power = .5)$v) + 1 + 1  #total required sample size
# if you have the t-value (ncp), and N (number of pairs)
